hypothesis: If a large language model is continually pre-trained on domain-specific chemical and MOF literature before supervised fine-tuning on property prediction tasks, then it will achieve lower mean absolute error in predicting MOF properties (band gap and gas adsorption) compared to baseline models and models without domain-specific pre-training, because exposure to MOF-relevant chemical knowledge during pre-training enables the model to develop richer representations of MOFid text inputs that capture structure-property relationships.
